title: calliandra haematomma var. pubescens
intro:
Calliandra haematomma var. pubescens, also known as the "Red Powder Puff," is a flowering plant species within the legume family, characterized by its vibrant red flowers and a powdery coating on its leaves and stems.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds and skin conditions, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.

The health benefits of Calliandra haematomma var. pubescens include its potential to accelerate wound healing, reduce inflammation, and even exhibit antifungal properties, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.

In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and stems are typically used to create teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be applied topically to the affected area or consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
